speaker: SILVIUS
text: Sweet Phoebe, do not scorn me. Do not, Phoebe.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Say that you love me not, but say not so

speaker: SILVIUS
text: In bitterness. The common executioner,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Whose heart th’ accustomed sight of death makes hard,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Falls not the axe upon the humbled neck

speaker: SILVIUS
text: But first begs pardon. Will you sterner be

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Than he that dies and lives by bloody drops?

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I would not be thy executioner.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I fly thee, for I would not injure thee.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Thou tell’st me there is murder in mine eye.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: ’Tis pretty, sure, and very probable

speaker: PHOEBE
text: That eyes, that are the frail’st and softest things,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Who shut their coward gates on atomies,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Should be called tyrants, butchers, murderers.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Now I do frown on thee with all my heart,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: And if mine eyes can wound, now let them kill thee.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Now counterfeit to swoon; why, now fall down;

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Or if thou canst not, O, for shame, for shame,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Lie not, to say mine eyes are murderers.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Now show the wound mine eye hath made in thee.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Scratch thee but with a pin, and there remains

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Some scar of it. Lean upon a rush,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: The cicatrice and capable impressure

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Thy palm some moment keeps. But now mine eyes,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Which I have darted at thee, hurt thee not;

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Nor I am sure there is no force in eyes

speaker: PHOEBE
text: That can do hurt.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: O dear Phoebe,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: If ever—as that ever may be near—

speaker: SILVIUS
text: You meet in some fresh cheek the power of fancy,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Then shall you know the wounds invisible

speaker: SILVIUS
text: That love’s keen arrows make.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: But till that time

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Come not thou near me. And when that time comes,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Afflict me with thy mocks, pity me not,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: As till that time I shall not pity thee.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And why, I pray you? Who might be your mother,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That you insult, exult, and all at once,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Over the wretched? What though you have no beauty—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: As, by my faith, I see no more in you

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Than without candle may go dark to bed—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Must you be therefore proud and pitiless?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Why, what means this? Why do you look on me?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I see no more in you than in the ordinary

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Of nature’s sale-work.—’Od’s my little life,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I think she means to tangle my eyes, too.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: No, faith, proud mistress, hope not after it.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: ’Tis not your inky brows, your black silk hair,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Your bugle eyeballs, nor your cheek of cream

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That can entame my spirits to your worship.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: You foolish shepherd, wherefore do you follow her,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Like foggy south puffing with wind and rain?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: You are a thousand times a properer man

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Than she a woman. ’Tis such fools as you

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That makes the world full of ill-favored children.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: ’Tis not her glass but you that flatters her,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And out of you she sees herself more proper

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Than any of her lineaments can show her.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: But, mistress, know yourself. Down on your knees

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And thank heaven, fasting, for a good man’s love,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: For I must tell you friendly in your ear,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Sell when you can; you are not for all markets.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Cry the man mercy, love him, take his offer.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Foul is most foul, being foul to be a scoffer.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: So take her to thee, shepherd. Fare you well.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Sweet youth, I pray you chide a year together.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I had rather hear you chide than this man woo.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: He’s fall’n in love with your

speaker: ROSALIND
text:
foulness.
                 
                (
                To
                 
                Silvius
                .
                )
               And she’ll fall in love with

speaker: ROSALIND
text: my anger. If it be so, as fast as she answers thee with

speaker: ROSALIND
text: frowning looks, I’ll sauce her with bitter words.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Why look you so upon me?

speaker: PHOEBE
text: For no ill will I bear you.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I pray you, do not fall in love with me,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: For I am falser than vows made in wine.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Besides, I like you not. If you will know my house,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: ’Tis at the tuft of olives, here hard by.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Will you go, sister?—Shepherd, ply her hard.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Come, sister.—Shepherdess, look on him better,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And be not proud. Though all the world could see,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: None could be so abused in sight as he.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Come, to our flock.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Dead shepherd, now I find thy saw of might:

speaker: PHOEBE
text: “Who ever loved that loved not at first sight?”

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Sweet Phoebe—

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Ha, what sayst thou, Silvius?

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Sweet Phoebe, pity me.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Why, I am sorry for thee, gentle Silvius.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Wherever sorrow is, relief would be.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: If you do sorrow at my grief in love,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: By giving love your sorrow and my grief

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Were both extermined.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Thou hast my love. Is not that neighborly?

speaker: SILVIUS
text: I would have you.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Why, that were covetousness.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Silvius, the time was that I hated thee;

speaker: PHOEBE
text: And yet it is not that I bear thee love;

speaker: PHOEBE
text: But since that thou canst talk of love so well,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Thy company, which erst was irksome to me,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I will endure, and I’ll employ thee too.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: But do not look for further recompense

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Than thine own gladness that thou art employed.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: So holy and so perfect is my love,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: And I in such a poverty of grace,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: That I shall think it a most plenteous crop

speaker: SILVIUS
text: To glean the broken ears after the man

speaker: SILVIUS
text: That the main harvest reaps. Loose now and then

speaker: SILVIUS
text: A scattered smile, and that I’ll live upon.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Know’st thou the youth that spoke to me erewhile?

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Not very well, but I have met him oft,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: And he hath bought the cottage and the bounds

speaker: SILVIUS
text: That the old carlot once was master of.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Think not I love him, though I ask for him.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: ’Tis but a peevish boy—yet he talks well—

speaker: PHOEBE
text: But what care I for words? Yet words do well

speaker: PHOEBE
text: When he that speaks them pleases those that hear.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: It is a pretty youth—not very pretty—

speaker: PHOEBE
text: But sure he’s proud—and yet his pride becomes him.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: He’ll make a proper man. The best thing in him

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Is his complexion; and faster than his tongue

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Did make offense, his eye did heal it up.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: He is not very tall—yet for his years he’s tall.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: His leg is but so-so—and yet ’tis well.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: There was a pretty redness in his lip,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: A little riper and more lusty red

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Than that mixed in his cheek: ’twas just the difference

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Betwixt the constant red and mingled damask.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: There be some women, Silvius, had they marked him

speaker: PHOEBE
text: In parcels as I did, would have gone near

speaker: PHOEBE
text: To fall in love with him; but for my part

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I love him not nor hate him not; and yet

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I have more cause to hate him than to love him.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: For what had he to do to chide at me?

speaker: PHOEBE
text: He said mine eyes were black and my hair black,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: And now I am remembered, scorned at me.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I marvel why I answered not again.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: But that’s all one: omittance is no quittance.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I’ll write to him a very taunting letter,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: And thou shalt bear it. Wilt thou, Silvius?

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Phoebe, with all my heart.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I’ll write it straight.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: The matter’s in my head and in my heart.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I will be bitter with him and passing short.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Go with me, Silvius.
